source_text: If the server supplies an empty Certificate message, the client MUST abort the handshake with a "decode_error" alert.
rule_statement: If the server sends an empty Certificate message, the client MUST abort with decode_error.
applies_when: Client receives empty Certificate message from server
checkability_rationale: Can verify client aborts when receiving empty server Certificate.

source_text: Any endpoint receiving any certificate which it would need to validate using any signature algorithm using an MD5 hash MUST abort the handshake with a "bad_certificate" alert.
rule_statement: Any endpoint receiving a certificate requiring MD5 validation MUST abort with bad_certificate.
applies_when: Endpoint receives certificate signed with MD5-based algorithm that needs validation
checkability_rationale: Can verify endpoint rejects MD5-signed certificates with correct alert.
ambiguity_notes: Self-signed or trust anchor certificates are exempt (not validated as part of chain).

source_text: SHA-1 is deprecated, and it is RECOMMENDED that any endpoint receiving any certificate which it would need to validate using any signature algorithm using a SHA-1 hash abort the handshake with a "bad_certificate" alert.
rule_statement: It is RECOMMENDED that endpoints abort with bad_certificate when receiving a certificate requiring SHA-1 validation.
applies_when: Endpoint receives certificate signed with SHA-1 that needs validation
checkability_rationale: Can verify endpoint rejects SHA-1 signed certificates.
ambiguity_notes: Self-signed or trust anchor certificates are exempt.
